question: Which university did the author of The Hobbit teach at?

context.title: J. R. R. Tolkien
context.text: J. R. R. Tolkien wrote The Hobbit and was a professor at Oxford University. He was a philologist and author.

context.title: Oxford University
context.text: Oxford University is a collegiate research university in Oxford, England. It is one of the oldest universities in the world.

question: What river flows through the city where Ada Lovelace was born?

context.title: Ada Lovelace

question: What instrument did the composer of The Four Seasons mainly play?

context.title: The Four Seasons
context.text: The Four Seasons is a group of violin concertos by Antonio Vivaldi. It is one of his most famous works.

context.title: Antonio Vivaldi
context.text: Antonio Vivaldi was an Italian Baroque composer and virtuoso violinist. He is known for The Four Seasons.

question: What ocean is named after the explorer who led the first expedition to circumnavigate it?

context.title: Christopher Columbus
context.text: Christopher Columbus was an Italian explorer. In 1492 he sailed across what was later named the Atlantic Ocean.

context.title: Atlantic Ocean
context.text: The Atlantic Ocean is named after Atlas, a figure from Greek mythology. It is the second-largest ocean.

question: Which sea is named after the color of its water, and borders the countries where both Julius Caesar and Cleopatra ruled?

context.title: Cleopatra
context.text: Cleopatra VII was the last active ruler of Ptolemaic Egypt. Egypt borders the Red Sea to the east.

context.title: Red Sea
context.text: The Red Sea is a body of water between Africa and Asia. It is called the Red Sea due to occasional blooms of red algae.

question: What mountain range contains the highest peak in South America?

context.title: Aconcagua
context.text: Aconcagua is the highest peak in South America. It is located in the Andes mountain range.

question: Who was the composer of a famous symphony that inspired the national anthem of a European country?

question: Which sea is named after the color of its water, and borders the countries where both Julius Caesar and Cleopatra ruled?

context.title: Cleopatra
context.text: Cleopatra VII was the last active ruler of Ptolemaic Egypt. Egypt borders the Red Sea to the east.

context.title: Red Sea
context.text: The Red Sea is a body of water between Africa and Asia. It is called the Red Sea due to occasional blooms of red algae.

question: In what year did the person who invented the telephone patent their first telephone?

context.title: Alexander Graham Bell
context.text: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with inventing the telephone. He was born in Scotland.

context.title: First Telephone Patent
context.text: The first telephone patent was filed in 1876. Alexander Graham Bell received U.S. Patent No. 174,465 for improvements in telephony.

question: What ocean is named after the explorer who led the first expedition to circumnavigate it?

context.title: Christopher Columbus
context.text: Christopher Columbus was an Italian explorer. In 1492 he sailed across what was later named the Atlantic Ocean.

context.title: Atlantic Ocean
context.text: The Atlantic Ocean is named after Atlas, a figure from Greek mythology. It is the second-largest ocean.

question: Which university did the author of The Hobbit teach at?

context.title: J. R. R. Tolkien
context.text: J. R. R. Tolkien wrote The Hobbit and was a professor at Oxford University. He was a philologist and author.

context.text: Oxford University is a collegiate research university in Oxford, England. It is one of the oldest universities in the world.

question: What mountain range contains the highest peak in South America?

context.title: Aconcagua
context.text: Aconcagua is the highest peak in South America. It is located in the Andes mountain range.

context.title: Andes
context.text: The Andes is a mountain range in South America. It is the longest mountain range in the world.

question: What instrument did the composer of The Four Seasons mainly play?

context.title: The Four Seasons
context.text: The Four Seasons is a group of violin concertos by Antonio Vivaldi. It is one of his most famous works.

context.title: Antonio Vivaldi
context.text: Antonio Vivaldi was an Italian Baroque composer and virtuoso violinist. He is known for The Four Seasons.

question: What river flows through the city where Ada Lovelace was born?

context.title: Ada Lovelace
context.text: Ada Lovelace was born in London, England in 1815. She was a mathematician and writer.

context.title: London
context.text: London is the capital of England. It is crossed by the River Thames which flows through the city.

question: Who was the composer of a famous symphony that inspired the national anthem of a European country?

context.text: The Russian national anthem was composed by Alexandrov in 1944, but was based on themes from earlier Russian composers.
